steps.title: Convert to a quadratic using substitution
steps.content:
Let $u = x^n$. The equation $(x^n)^2 = 2x^n + 35$ becomes:
$$u^2 = 2u + 35$$
Rearranging to standard form:
$$u^2 - 2u - 35 = 0$$
Factoring (looking for two numbers that multiply to $-35$ and add to $-2$, which are $-7$ and \$5$):
$$(u - 7)(u + 5) = 0$$
This gives $u = 7$ or $u = -5$.

steps.title: Take the positive solution
steps.content: Since $u = x^n$, taking the positive root gives $x^n = 7$.
